behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all key paths (src/server/services/email/impls/index.ts, src/envs/email.ts, AGENTS.md, README.md), the EmailImplType enum, literal values ('resend', 'nodemailer'), env var names (RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_FROM, EMAIL_SERVICE_PROVIDER), class patterns, and documentation headings.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to add and references patterns rather than specifying exact code lines; agent must still design the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert specific naming like 'class ResendImpl', 'EmailImplType.Resend', 'new ResendImpl()', and 'Resend = \'resend\'' regex — alternative valid names (e.g., ResendProvider, EmailImplType.RESEND) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most f2p tests are string-inclusion checks on source files (e.g., code.includes('class ResendImpl'), 'EMAIL_SERVICE_PROVIDER' in code); no test actually invokes sendMail or constructs EmailService to verify behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific structural elements (class, interface implementation, enum value, factory case) that a no-op stub without real changes couldn't satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: README documentation, AGENTS.md section, env vars, factory registration, and provider selection logic are all tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by clearly labeled section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Config/documentation).

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces TS code and markdown, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and identifiers appear consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction references files naturally by their role; no line-level hints.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Gold patch to AGENTS.md and README.md adds localized sections rather than rewriting files.
